mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer spending and residential investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with >55% accuracy, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening economic activity and higher capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week, but only during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of rising INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth coincides with negative equity returns in >58% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL acceleration, yielding positive 60-day returns in >65% of triggered instances, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with a declining federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of rising GDPC1 and falling FEDFUNDS fails to predict positive returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden contraction in industrial production during a period of rising unemployment signals a severe supply-side shock and recessionary pressure, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to produce negative 5-day returns in >55% of recessionary trough observations, or the joint deterioration coincides with equity rallies.
